summary: McKenzie describes his process of upgrading the Buy Now and Download Free Trial buttons on Bingo Card Creator's website. He purchased Axialis Icon Workshop on community recommendations to create Web 2.0-style buttons, replacing his earlier Paint.NET creations. The post shows before-and-after comparisons and frames the redesign as an A/B test to see if newer button styles convert better. It's a brief, practical update typical of his early blogging about incremental improvements to his software business.
key_points: Upgraded call-to-action buttons from basic Paint.NET designs to polished Web 2.0 style using Axialis Icon Workshop
Framed the visual redesign explicitly as a conversion experiment to measure whether new buttons sell better
Leveraged community recommendations (Business of Software forum) for tooling decisions
Even small UI elements like buttons were treated as optimization opportunities worth investing in
money_quotes: I decided to redo my sort of frumpy Buy Now! and Download Free Trial buttons for Bingo Card Creator, and see if the new versions aren't any more successful.
key_insight: Even minor visual elements like purchase buttons deserve deliberate design investment and testing, because they sit at the critical point of conversion.
